summary.intent: Implement a lazy tool registry feature that shows a single `tool_search` meta-tool instead of all tool schemas per LLM turn, reducing token usage from 15,000+ tokens when many MCP servers are connected.
summary.outcome: All four phases implemented and merged into PR #467: ToolRegistry plumbing with activated map, ToolSearchTool with keyword/activation search, config wiring via `registry_mode = "lazy"`, system prompt hints, and e2e test fixes for pre-existing sandbox test failures unrelated to the feature.

repo: Local `main` is 1566 commits ahead of `origin/main` — this is a fork/private repo setup where local history significantly diverges from remote; PRs must be rebased onto `origin/main` before pushing
The `beads`/dolt server for issue tracking is not reliably available in the dev environment

Sandbox e2e tests depend on `window.__MOLTIS__` (gon data) embedded in the initial HTML; when `sandbox.backend` is `'none'`, the Refresh button title changes to a disabled hint which breaks Playwright `getByRole` accessible name matching

Playwright `route.fetch()` in a `beforeEach` bootstrap mock can throw 'Target page has been closed' if the test completes before the in-flight route callback finishes — requires `afterEach` with `page.unrouteAll({ behavior: 'ignoreErrors' })`
`ToolRegistry::get()` was changed to return `Arc<dyn AgentTool>` instead of `&dyn AgentTool`; callers in gateway and chat crates needed migration from `get_arc()` to `get()`

The chat integration point for wrapping the registry is in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` around line 5994, after `apply_runtime_tool_filters()` and `install_agent_scoped_memory_tools()`, using `persona.config` for the config reference

code.finding: Added `ActivatedTools = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<dyn AgentTool>>>>` field; uses `std::sync::Mutex` (not tokio) because lock is held for microseconds and `list_schemas()` must remain sync
`tool_schemas`/`schemas_for_api` computation moved inside both runner loops (non-streaming and streaming) so activated tools appear on the next iteration after `tool_search` activates them

NEW file: `ToolSearchTool` with scoring (exact name=100, name contains=50, word overlap=10×count), max 15 results; `wrap_registry_lazy()` creates empty registry with only `tool_search`, sharing the activated Arc with the full registry
Integration point: wrap registry with `wrap_registry_lazy` when `persona.config.tools.registry_mode == ToolRegistryMode::Lazy`
Bootstrap route mock using `route.fetch()` needs `afterEach` cleanup with `page.unrouteAll({ behavior: 'ignoreErrors' })` to avoid 'Target page closed' errors on fast-completing tests

workflow: When rebasing a branch from a diverged local main onto origin/main, check commit count with `git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD` after rebase — the rebase warnings about skipped commits are harmless if the count is correct
PR review comments can be resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation using thread node IDs from `gh api graphql` — comment node IDs and thread node IDs are different
Playwright `page.evaluate(fetch(...))` calls bypass route mocking since they run in the browser context against the real server; mock those endpoints with `page.route` + `route.fulfill` instead

When mocking gon/bootstrap data in Playwright, `page.addInitScript` alone is insufficient if the app reads `window.__MOLTIS__` at module load time — intercepting the network response with `route.fetch()` + modified JSON is more reliable

friction: Local main being 1566 commits ahead of origin/main caused PR creation to fail silently; required rebase onto origin/main before gh pr create worked
Beads/dolt issue tracking server was unavailable, so task tracking had to be done manually
Merge conflict on sandboxes.spec.js when merging origin/main — origin/main had similar (but slightly different) sandbox mock fixes that overlapped with the branch's changes

Sandbox Playwright tests failed due to `sandboxRuntimeAvailable()` returning false in CI (no Docker daemon), causing button accessible names to change — required multiple iterations to diagnose (initScript → route mock → unrouteAll)
PR #330 was too large (72K additions) and had to be rewritten from scratch as a focused extraction
open_items: Manual QA not yet done: set `registry_mode = 'lazy'` in moltis.toml, connect MCP server, verify model uses tool_search to discover and call tools
Token comparison benchmarking deferred: count input tokens per turn in full vs lazy mode with 50+ MCP tools
PR strategy split (PR 1 = Phases 1+2 dead code, PR 2 = Phases 3+4 user-facing) was collapsed into a single PR — the intended incremental PR approach was not followed
Sub-agents don't inherit activated tools (fresh empty `activated` on clone) — this is intentional but may need revisiting if sub-agent tool discovery is needed
`max_iterations * 3` multiplier for lazy mode is hardcoded — could be made configurable
